title: A road sign in Tashkent I couldn't read
summary: A short story about a single sign in a foreign script that became part of your daily life.
topic: A road sign in Tashkent I couldn't read
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a sign in a foreign alphabet you saw every day for a long time without understanding. Describe the sign, when you finally read it, and what it said. Use past simple and past continuous.
hintStarters: Every morning for a month in…, I walked past the same blue sign on…
I could not read a single letter of it because…
On a slow Saturday, I asked a young man at the café next door…
sampleText: Every morning for a month in Tashkent, I walked past the same blue sign on the corner of my street. It was painted in white Uzbek letters with a small picture of a bicycle next to them. I could not read a single letter because the alphabet was still new to me and I was studying very slowly. For two weeks I imagined the sign said something poetic, perhaps 'Always look both ways' or 'Slow down for children.' By the third week, I had built a small story around it in my head. On a slow Saturday, I finally asked a young man at the café next door if he could translate the sign for me. He looked at it, then looked at me, and said simply, 'Bicycle parking.' I started laughing. He looked worried, so I tried to explain that I was laughing at myself, not at his city. After that, I read more Uzbek street signs every week. Most of them said ordinary things — pharmacy, bakery, dental clinic, no parking. But I missed the version of the blue sign that had lived only in my head. Sometimes the words we cannot read are the ones we love the most.

có lẽ 'Luôn nhìn cả hai phía'

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Anh ấy nhìn nó, rồi nhìn tôi

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect for an earlier process
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I had built a small story around it in my head' dùng past perfect vì việc dựng truyện đã hoàn thành trước thời điểm tôi đi hỏi (past simple).
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I had built a small story around it in my head' uses past perfect because the storytelling was completed before the past moment of asking (past simple).

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'Could not' + base verb for past ability
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I could not read a single letter' = ở quá khứ tôi không có khả năng đọc. Sau modal 'could/couldn't' luôn dùng động từ nguyên mẫu.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I could not read a single letter' = past inability. After the modal 'could/couldn't', use the base verb.

sampleTranslationVi: Mỗi sáng trong một tháng ở Tashkent, tôi đi qua cùng tấm biển xanh ở góc phố nhà tôi. Nó sơn bằng chữ Uzbek màu trắng với một hình xe đạp nhỏ bên cạnh. Tôi không thể đọc nổi một chữ cái nào vì bảng chữ cái vẫn còn mới với tôi và tôi đang học rất chậm. Trong hai tuần tôi tưởng tượng tấm biển nói điều gì đó đầy thi vị, có lẽ 'Luôn nhìn cả hai phía' hoặc 'Đi chậm vì trẻ em.' Đến tuần thứ ba, tôi đã dựng một câu chuyện nhỏ quanh nó trong đầu. Vào một thứ Bảy thong thả, cuối cùng tôi hỏi một thanh niên ở quán cà phê bên cạnh liệu anh ấy có thể dịch tấm biển cho tôi không. Anh ấy nhìn nó, rồi nhìn tôi, và chỉ nói, 'Chỗ đỗ xe đạp.' Tôi bắt đầu cười. Anh ấy có vẻ lo, nên tôi cố giải thích rằng tôi đang cười chính mình, không phải cười thành phố của anh ấy. Sau đó, tôi đọc thêm nhiều biển hiệu tiếng Uzbek mỗi tuần. Hầu hết chúng nói những điều bình thường — hiệu thuốc, tiệm bánh, phòng khám răng, cấm đỗ. Nhưng tôi nhớ phiên bản tấm biển xanh mà chỉ tồn tại trong đầu tôi. Đôi khi những từ chúng ta không thể đọc lại là những từ chúng ta yêu nhất.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple sentence about a habit.
options: Every morning for a month, I walked past the same blue sign.
Every morning for a month, I walk past the same blue sign.
Every morning for a month, I am walking past the same blue sign.
Every morning for a month, I have walked past the same blue sign.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'walked' for a repeated past action over a finite period.

options: I could not read a single letter.
I could not to read a single letter.
I could not reading a single letter.
I could not read a single letters.
to express something written or said in another language
to change a sign's colour
to take a photo of a sign
to fix a broken sign

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the natural past continuous sentence.
options: I was studying very slowly.
I studying very slowly.
I was studying very slow.
I am studying very slowly.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous 'was studying'; adverb 'slowly' for manner.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past perfect sentence.
options: I had built a small story around it in my head.
I have build a small story around it in my head.
I was build a small story around it in my head.
I had build a small story around it in my head.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect 'had built' for an earlier completed action.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the natural sentence with 'if + could' (indirect question).
options: I asked him if he could translate the sign for me.
I asked him if he can translate the sign for me.
I asked him if could he translate the sign for me.
I asked him that if he could translate the sign for me.
mcQuestions.explanation: Reported indirect question: 'asked + if + statement word order' with backshift to 'could'.

options: normal, not special
very strange
expensive
secret

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence with 'laugh at'.
options: I was laughing at myself, not at his city.
I was laughing on myself, not on his city.
I was laughing to myself, not to his city.
I was laughing for myself, not for his city.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Laugh at + object' is the natural collocation.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct preposition: 'on the corner ___ my street'.
options: of
in
at
from
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Corner of (the street)' is the standard pattern.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence with 'most of'.
options: Most of them said ordinary things.
Most of them said ordinary thing.
Most them said ordinary things.
The most of them said ordinary things.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Most of + pronoun' followed by past simple plural noun.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural closing sentence.
options: Sometimes the words we cannot read are the ones we love the most.
Sometimes the words that we cannot read them are the ones we love the most.
Sometimes the words we cannot read is the ones we love the most.
Sometimes the words we cannot read are the one we love the most.
mcQuestions.explanation: Reduced relative clause 'we cannot read' modifies 'words'; subject-verb agreement (plural words → are); 'the ones' refers back to plural words.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why are imagined translations of foreign signs often more beautiful than the real ones? What do we lose when we finally learn the language?
